LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT LAWRENCE WONG'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION. THE APPLICABLE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS FOR FRAUD IS 3 YEARS PER CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE 338(D). PLAINTIFF FAILS TO ALLEGE FACTS SHOWING THAT THE DISCOVERY RULE OR EQUITABLE TOLLING APPLIES TO HIS FRAUD CLAIM. IN ORDER TO SHOW THAT THE DISCOVERY RULE APPLIES, PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE THE TIME AND MANNER OF DISCOVERY AND THE INABILITY TO HAVE MADE EARLIER DISCOVERY DESPITE REASONABLE DILIGENCE. FURTHERMORE, THE FRAUD CLAIM IS NOT ALLEGED WITH THE SUFFICIENT SPECIFICITY. AMENDED COMPLAINT TO BE FILED BY DECEMBER 19, 2011. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077 =302/HEK

Defendant Lawrence Wong'S Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Respond To Discovery Requests
Off Calendar Jan-11-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for hearing on defendant Lawrence Wong's motion to compel plaintiff Kin Fong to respond to discovery requests.
Dept. 220, Judge: James J. McBride, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Not Reported.

In Pro Per plaintiff Kin Fong appeared via telephone.
Michael Mutalipassi, Esq. appeared for defendant Lawrence Wong, who was not present at today's hearing.

Prior to today's hearing, the parties were Ordered by Judge Miller to meet & confer on all discovery disputes at issue.

After hearing oral argument by plaintiff and defense counsel, and good appearing therfor, defendant's motion to compel plaintiff to respond to discovery requests is GRANTED.

On or before January 25, 2012, plaintiff Kin Fong shall provide defendant Lawrence Wong with complete and fully responsive answers to defendant's special interrogatories and form interrogatories, without objections.

On or before January 25, 2012, plaintiff shall provide defendant with complete and fully responsive answers to defendant's request for production of documents, without objections.

On or before February 1, 2012, plaintiff shall provide the documents responsive to defendant's request for production of documents.

The Court further Orders that plaintiff Kin Fong shall pay defendant Lawrence Wong sanctions in the amount of $2,000.00. Said amount is payable on or before February 1, 2012.

Counsel for defendant shall prepare the Order After Hearing.

Off Calendar Dec-09-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - KIN K. FONG, TEL. NO. 863-0667, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
ATTY. MICHAEL MUTALIPASI OF WONG & ASSOCIATES, TEL. NO.(510) 451-2124 SPECIALLY APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT EARL JIANG'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is Sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the 1st Cause of action. The applicable statute of limitations for fraud is 3 years per Code of Civil Procedure 338(d). Plaintiff fails to allege facts showing that the discovery rule or equitable tolling applies to his fraud claim. In order to show that the discovery rule applies, plaintiff must allege the time and manner of discovery and the inability to have made earlier discovery despite reasonable diligence. Furthermore, the fraud claim is not alleged with the sufficient specificity. AMENDED COMPLAINT TO BE FILED BY DECEMBER 19, 2011. Prevailing party to prepare order. Judge: Harold Kahn, Court Reporter: Lavena Ward, CSR #7077=(302/HEK)
